prompt: Write a story about a detective in 1920s Chicago who discovers that the speakeasy she has been investigating is actually a front for a secret society of time travelers. Include rich period details, multiple characters with distinct voices, plot twists, and at least three chapters. Chapter 1: The Gin Joint on Halsted Street

result.text: Detective Olivia Clark had never quite fit in with her colleagues at the Chicago Police Department. She was bright and sharp, but she just couldn't seem to get a break, no matter how hard she tried. But when a high-profile murder case drew her into the midst of Chi-town's underworld, Olivia knew it could be her lucky day. This time it seemed as if fate had favored her because the evidence in the murder room was worth more than any loot ever held by gangsters or hardened detectives. The victim was an old friend and business partner of long standing, a wealthy real estate developer who'd been killed within hours of making what appeared to be a profitable investment deal with his lover. Olivia took the case seriously, but her fellow investigators doubted that it could have been one single murder—especially since there were no obvious motives or witnesses. They thought she was making a mountain out of a molehill by trying to crack into an era too steeped in darkness for anything ever to be seen. Olivia wasn't convinced they were blind. She decided the case would be worth pursuing, and even if it only led her down dead ends, that would be better than disappointment...
